prompt: Strict realistic editorial photograph, wide 16:9, rear three-quarter view under a home flight simulator desk. Val Chen, an attractive East Asian woman simulator engineer with shoulder-length dark hair, is kneeling and visibly attaching colored cable labels to real USB, HDMI and power cables coming from the backs of two rectangular generic glass-cockpit display panels mounted on the desktop above. Clearly show the cable bundle, powered USB hub, strain relief, and her hand holding a label maker. The product action dominates the frame. Bright daylight aerospace workshop, turquoise wall, orange tool case, correct human anatomy and cable scale. Absolutely no aircraft engine, no server rack, no logos, no readable screen UI, no collage, no typography.

Candid wide editorial photo in a daylight flight training room. Val Chen, confident attractive East Asian female simulator engineer, sits in a realistic general-aviation simulator and flies a procedure using two generic glass avionics panels while an instructor watches from the side. Her right hand turns a bezel knob and left hand rests on the yoke, focused natural expression. Bright sky-blue, white and safety-orange palette, believable cockpit geometry, no product logos, no legible fake UI, no collage, no typography.

Close but wide workshop editorial photograph. Val Chen, attractive East Asian simulator curator wearing a coral utility jacket, uses digital calipers and a steel ruler to compare panel depth, VESA clearance and cable bend radius on a generic glass cockpit module. The bench is colorful and organized with green cutting mat and yellow cable ties. She is doing real engineering work, not posing. Realistic hands, bright neutral light, no logos, no readable text, no collage, no typography.

Wide documentary photo in a modern simulator lab. Wide side-view editorial photograph. Strict realistic wide workbench photograph. Candid wide editorial photograph in a home flight-sim room.
